[Watch Now](https://finance.yahoo.com/live/).","score":0.2258436,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SpaceX","title":"SpaceX","content":"- 1.3.1 Commercial launches and rapid growth. + 1.4 2019–2020: Raising capital, Starlink launch, and crewed launches. - 1.5.1 Starlink expansion. - 1.5.2 Starlink in the Russo-Ukrainian war. - 1.5.4 xAI acquisition, Terafab, and Starfall. + 4.1 Cargo to International Space Station (ISS)). **Space Exploration Technologies Corp.**, doing business as **SpaceX**, is an American spaceflight, telecommunications, and artificial intelligence company headquartered at the Starbase development site in Starbase, Texas. In 2010, the company launched the Falcon 9 launch vehicle and the Dragon 1 spacecraft to fulfill NASA's Commercial Orbital Transportation Services (COTS) contracts for cargo deliveries to the International Space Station (ISS). In 2012, SpaceX began flying Commercial Resupply Services missions to the ISS, becoming the first private company to successfully dock with the station, and started developing technologies to make the Falcon 9 first stage reusable.","score":0.16106966,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.34,"request_id":"88812f0d-6651-45d4-b0a4-23580688592f"}
